steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The claim states Winnemucca addresses readers “to establish her authority and credibility” — the purpose is crucial. The winning quotation must assert her knowledge, experience, or trustworthiness, not merely greet or narrate to readers.

steps.title: Apply the signal
steps.content: Choice A directly claims authority: “I have not lived in this world for over thirty or forty years for nothing, and I know what I am talking about.” This establishes her credibility by asserting her knowledge based on experience. The other choices either undermine credibility with uncertainty or fail to assert authority at all. This matches choice A — A is correct.
